Source: AgentCore Developer Guide
URL: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock-agentcore/latest/devguide/what-is-bedrock-agentcore.html
Fetched: 2026-03-22

# Amazon Bedrock AgentCore - Overview

## What is Amazon Bedrock AgentCore?

Amazon Bedrock AgentCore is an agentic platform for building, deploying, and operating highly effective agents securely at scale using any framework and foundation model. With AgentCore, you can:

- Enable agents to take actions across tools and data with the right permissions and governance
- Run agents securely at scale
- Monitor agent performance and quality in production
- All without any infrastructure management

AgentCore services work together or independently with any open-source framework (CrewAI, LangGraph, LlamaIndex, Strands Agents) and with any foundation model, providing open-source flexibility with enterprise-grade security and reliability.

## Core Services in Amazon Bedrock AgentCore

| Service | Description | Integrations |
|---------|-------------|--------------|
| **Runtime** | Secure, serverless runtime environment for deploying and scaling dynamic AI agents and tools. Provides fast cold starts, extended runtime support for asynchronous agents, session isolation, built-in identity, and multi-modal/multi-agent support. | CrewAI, LangGraph, LlamaIndex, Google ADK, OpenAI Agents SDK, Strands Agents; OpenAI, Google Gemini, Anthropic Claude, Amazon Nova, Meta Llama, Mistral models; MCP and A2A protocols |
| **Memory** | Build context-aware agents with control over what they remember and learn. Supports short-term memory for multi-turn conversations and long-term memory persisting across sessions. | LangGraph, LangChain, Strands, LlamaIndex |
| **Gateway** | Convert APIs, Lambda functions, and existing services into Model Context Protocol (MCP)-compatible tools. | APIs, MCP tools, Lambda, Salesforce, Zoom, JIRA, Slack, etc. |
| **Identity** | Secure, scalable agent identity, access and authentication management compatible with existing identity providers. | Amazon Cognito, Okta, Microsoft Azure Entra ID, Auth0 |
| **Code Interpreter** | Isolated sandbox environment for agents to execute code. | Python, JavaScript, TypeScript |
| **Browser** | Fast, secure cloud-based browser runtime for agents to interact with web applications. | Playwright, BrowserUse frameworks |
| **Observability** | Unified view to trace, debug, and monitor agent performance in production with detailed visualizations. | OpenTelemetry (OTEL)-compatible formats |
| **Evaluations** | Automated, consistent, data-driven agent assessment service measuring task execution quality and reliability. | Strands Agent, LangGraph frameworks; OpenTelemetry or OpenInference instrumentation; CloudWatch integration |
| **Policy** | Deterministic control ensuring agents operate within defined boundaries and business rules using natural language or Cedar policy language. | Integrates with AgentCore Gateway |

## What Can You Build with Amazon Bedrock AgentCore?

- **Agents**: Autonomous AI apps that reason, use tools, and maintain context for customer support, workflow automation, data analysis, or coding assistance
- **Tools and MCP Servers**: Transform existing APIs, databases, or services into tools accessible to any MCP-compatible agent
- **Agent Platforms**: Provide internal developers or customers with a paved path to build and deploy agents with approved tools, shared memory, and governed access

## Pricing

AgentCore offers flexible, consumption-based pricing with no upfront commitments or minimum fees. For details, visit [AgentCore pricing](https://aws.amazon.com/bedrock/agentcore/pricing/).

Note: AgentCore may use and store your content to improve service experience or performance for your use only, not for other customers.

## Next Steps

- Get started with Amazon Bedrock AgentCore
- Understand the available interfaces for using Amazon Bedrock AgentCore
